title: Labeling of molecular probes with nonmetal and metalloid isotopes for multiplexed high resolution analysis
abstract: Provided herein, among other things, is a labeling moiety for the analysis of biological samples. In some embodiments, the labeling moiety comprises multiple internal atoms of a non-biological stable nonmetal or metalloid isotope, wherein: i. the labeling moiety is or comprises a binding agent that specifically binds to a complementary site in or on a cell, or ii. the labeling moiety comprises a chemoselective group. In either case, the labeling moiety is not an anti-IgG antibody. Methods for using the labeling moiety, and multiplexed mixture of the same, are also provided.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A labeling moiety, wherein the labeling moiety comprises multiple internal atoms of a non-biological stable nonmetal or metalloid isotope, wherein: i. the labeling moiety is or comprises a binding agent that specifically binds to or reacts with a complementary site in or on a cell, or ii. the labeling moiety comprises a chemo selective group, wherein the labeling moiety is not an anti-IgG antibody. 2. The labeling moiety of claim 1, wherein the labeling moiety is or comprises a binding agent that specifically binds to a complementary site in or on a cell. 3. The labeling moiety of claim 2, wherein the binding agent is an antibody. 4. The labeling moiety of claim 1, wherein the labeling moiety is a polymer that has a chemo selective group. 5. The labeling moiety of claim 4, wherein the polymer is an oligonucleotide. 6. The labeling moiety of claim 5, wherein the chemoselective group is amine -reactive, thiol reactive or hydroxyl reactive. 7. The labeling moiety of claim 1, wherein the labeling moiety is composed of a polymer and a binding agent that specifically binds to a complementary site in or on a cell, wherein only the polymer comprises the atoms of the stable nonmetal or metalloid isotope. 8. The labeling moiety of claim 7, wherein the labeling moiety comprises an antibody conjugated to an oligonucleotide that comprises atoms of a stable nonmetal or metalloid isotope. 9. The labeling moiety of claim 1, wherein herein the labeling moiety is or comprises a nucleic acid that specifically binds to a protein in a sequence-specific manner. 10. The labeling moiety of claim 1, wherein herein the labeling moiety is a transposase complex. 11. The labeling moiety of any prior claim, wherein the labeling moiety comprises a stable nonmetal isotope of C, N, O, F, P, S, Cl, Se, Br, or I. 12. The labeling moiety of any prior claim, wherein the moiety comprises a stable isotope of a halogen. 13. The labeling moiety of any prior claim, wherein the labeling moiety comprises a stable metalloid isotope of B, Si, Ge, As, Sb or Te. 14. The labeling moiety of any of any prior claim, wherein the moiety comprises two or more different stable nonmetal or metalloid isotopes. 15. The labeling moiety of any prior claim, wherein the moiety comprises: (a) a primary oligonucleotide and (b) a labeled oligonucleotide that contains one or more contains non-metal/metalloid isotopes, where the primary oligonucleotide comprises: (i) a first, target- specific, sequence that is complementary to a sequence in a cell, and (ii) a second sequence that is complementary to the labeled oligonucleotide. 16. A set of labeling moieties of any of claims 1-15, wherein the moieties are distinguishably labeled by the stable nonmetal isotopes. 17. The set of labeling moieties of claim 15, wherein the moieties are in separate tubes. 18. The set of labeling moieties of claim 15, wherein the moieties are mixed. 19. The set of labeling moieties of any of claims 16-18, wherein the moieties comprises multiple internal atoms of at least two stable nonmetal or metalloid isotopes, wherein the moieties are distinguishable by the identity and/or ratio of the different isotopes. 20. A method of generating a high resolution image by secondary ion mass spectrometry (SIMS), comprising: incubating the sample with one or more labeling moieties of claims 1-19, thereby producing a labeled sample in which a biological feature of interest is bound to the one or more labeling moieties; scanning the sample by secondary ion mass spectrometry (SIMS) using a positively charged ion beam to generate a data set that comprises spatially-addressable measurements of the abundance of said at least one stable nonmetal or metalloid isotope across said sample; and outputting the data set. 21. A method of analyzing cells by mass cytometry, comprising: incubating the cells with one or more labeling moieties of claims 1-19, thereby producing a labeled sample in which the labeling moieties are bound to the cells; analyzing the cells by mass cytometry, using a positively charged plasma beam to atomize the cells and generate a data set that comprises spatially-addressable measurements of the abundance of the stable nonmetal or metalloid isotopes in or on the cells; and outputting the data set.
